regions.eastern-caribbean: Eastern Caribbean weather for March 31, 2026: San Juan and nearby ports including St. Thomas, St. Croix, St. Maarten, St. Kitts, Antigua, and Turks & Caicos will experience unsettled conditions with moderate easterly winds increasing to 10-13 knots. Expect frequent rain showers and a high chance of rain through the next 72 hours, with periods of reduced visibility and wet decks likely. Mariners should secure loose gear and prepare for occasional gusts but no small craft advisories are currently in effect. Passengers should carry rain gear and plan for limited outdoor activities, while fishing and yacht captains are advised to monitor localized squalls especially near exposed anchorages such as Charlotte Amalie and Philipsburg.
regions.western-caribbean: Western Caribbean brief for March 31, 2026: Cozumel and neighboring ports including Costa Maya, Roatan, Belize City, Grand Cayman, Ocho Rios, and Falmouth are experiencing light easterly winds 10-12 knots with mostly stable conditions today. Expect mostly overcast skies tomorrow with a slight chance of showers near coastal areas, increasing rain chances to around 40% by April 2, accompanied by thunderstorms and easterly winds up to 13 knots. Mariners should prepare for localized heavy rain and possible thunderstorm activity by day three, which may reduce visibility and cause gusty winds; small craft operators should remain vigilant, especially when operating near exposed anchorages in Roatan and Belize City. Cruise and fishing captains are advised to monitor updated forecasts and schedule outdoor activities accordingly, as conditions are generally safe today but deteriorate midweek.
regions.bahamas: Bahamas region weather today is calm and stable with light ENE winds at 8 knots and partly cloudy skies. Conditions remain favorable for all maritime operations, including fishing and yacht anchoring in Nassau, Freeport, Bimini, Berry Islands, and Turks & Caicos, with negligible rain chances. Over the next three days, expect increasing ENE to E winds building to 20-21 knots with light drizzle likely; small craft advisories may be warranted by Day 2 and Day 3 for vessels sensitive to wind and sea state. Captains should prepare for choppier conditions and reduced visibility during these periods, while cruise passengers should plan for intermittent wet weather and overcast skies.
regions.southern-caribbean: Southern Caribbean ports remain under stable trade wind flow with easterly winds 13-15 knots and overcast skies. Aruba, Curacao, Bonaire, and Cartagena see a 40-50% chance of scattered showers today, tapering to 25% by Wednesday, with temperatures steady near 80 degrees Fahrenheit. Mariners should expect moderate chop and localized rain squalls; small craft should exercise caution near exposed anchorages, especially around Cartagena’s outer harbor where passing showers may reduce visibility briefly. Cruise and fishing operators should advise passengers to carry rain gear and secure loose items topside as passing showers are likely but no severe weather or advisories are expected.
regions.central-caribbean: Central Caribbean conditions are stable this morning with Grand Cayman reporting 78°F, east-northeast winds at 15 knots, and partly cloudy skies with no rain expected. Over the next three days, expect increasing cloud cover and a rise in shower activity, with thunderstorms possible by April 2. Mariners and yacht captains should monitor weather updates before transits, especially near Belize City and Roatan where easterly winds may increase wave action; no Small Craft Advisories are in effect at this time. Cruise passengers should plan for intermittent rain and brief thunderstorms late in the period, with outdoor activities best scheduled for early daytime hours.
regions.lesser-antilles: Lesser Antilles briefing for March 31, 2026: Barbados reports steady ENE winds at 10-12 knots with overcast skies and minimal rain risk today, increasing slightly to scattered showers by midweek. Trade winds from the east to ENE will keep seas moderate with no Small Craft Advisories expected across the region. Fishing captains should plan for stable conditions but expect localized showers near Martinique and St. Lucia by Day 2, which could reduce visibility and affect catch rates. Yacht operators anchoring at sheltered harbors like St. Vincent and Grenada are advised to check for gusts near showers and secure vessels accordingly. Passengers should carry light rain gear for intermittent showers but anticipate mostly comfortable temperatures in the low 80s.
regions.los-angeles: Los Angeles and nearby ports are experiencing cool, overcast conditions with light south-southwest winds near 5 knots and minimal rain risk today. Expect similar overcast skies with slightly stronger southwest winds 9-14 knots over the next three days, with only isolated light showers possible. Mariners operating out of Los Angeles, Long Beach, Marina del Rey, and Catalina Island should find generally stable conditions with no advisories in effect; moderate winds and overcast skies may reduce visibility somewhat, so maintain vigilant lookout and update navigation systems accordingly. Cruise and fishing captains should anticipate mild seas and plan decks and passenger activities to accommodate overcast skies and occasional light drizzle.
regions.ensenada: Ensenada region conditions for March 31, 2026 show persistent fog with light northwest winds at 3 knots and clear skies for the day, temperatures rising to near 69°F. Winds will shift west at 9-15 knots over the next three days, with increasing overcast skies and a low risk of light rain up to 13 percent. Mariners should expect reduced visibility near Ensenada and Punta Banda early today; maintain vigilant navigation and use radar where available. Anchorage at Islas Todos Santos remains sheltered from the prevailing westerlies, but small craft operators should monitor wind increases by day three for comfortable maneuvering. Passengers advised to dress in layers due to cool mornings and mild afternoons.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Cabo San Lucas and surrounding ports will see mild conditions today with temperatures near 75°F, NNW winds at 8 knots, and clear skies. Expect increasing northwest winds up to 15-19 knots over the next two days with partly cloudy to overcast skies but no rain. Mariners should note that wind speeds remain below Small Craft Advisory thresholds, but fishing captains should secure lighter gear as seas may build slightly with the breeze. Anchorage in La Paz remains calm and suitable for overnight stays, while yacht operators should monitor wind shifts especially near San Jose del Cabo where gusts could reach upper teens in knots.
regions.mazatlan: Mazatlan and nearby ports Topolobampo and Altata will experience mild conditions today with light southwest winds near 1 knot and partly cloudy skies. Expect a gentle west to west-northwest breeze increasing to around 9-11 knots over the next three days, with mostly dry conditions and no significant rain threat. Mariners and fishing captains should find calm seas suitable for safe anchorage and operations, though vessels should monitor wind shifts as westerly winds pick up. Cruise passengers can enjoy comfortable temperatures near 77 to 78 degrees Fahrenheit with minimal weather disruptions.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Puerto Vallarta region currently reports light NNE winds at 3 knots, 79°F temperature, and partly cloudy skies with no rain expected. Through March 31, expect overcast conditions and light WNW winds at 8 knots, with a minimal 5% chance of rain mainly offshore. Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala will see similar mild conditions, favorable for small craft and fishing operations. Mariners should maintain routine vigilance but no advisories apply; anchorage remains calm with good visibility for safe navigation and passenger activities.
regions.western-mediterranean: Western Mediterranean briefing for March 31, 2026: Today’s conditions across key ports including Barcelona, Valencia, Palma de Mallorca, Ibiza, Malaga, Cadiz, Lisbon, and Gibraltar remain mild with light southeast to south-southwest winds 3 to 10 knots. Temperatures will rise gradually from upper 50s to mid-60s Fahrenheit by midweek. Expect partly cloudy skies today with minimal rain chances near 15%, increasing to scattered rain showers and fog by midweek, especially around Barcelona and Valencia. Mariners should anticipate good visibility and calm seas today, but fishing captains and yacht operators should prepare for reduced visibility and slick decks by Tuesday due to fog and rain. Cruise passengers should carry light rain gear starting Tuesday and plan for limited outdoor activities on day three as showers become more frequent in southern and western anchorages. No small craft advisories or gale warnings are expected at this time.
regions.central-mediterranean: Central Mediterranean weather remains unsettled March 31, 2026, with widespread rain and moderate northeast winds. Naples reports steady rain at 50°F with NE winds 8 knots, increasing to 14-16 knots with showers through Day 1 and Day 2. Expect similar wet conditions in nearby ports including Civitavecchia, Genoa, and Livorno, with rougher seas near exposed Sardinia and Corsica. Yacht and fishing captains should secure vessels and avoid exposed anchorages; cruise passengers advised to plan indoor activities and expect wet decks and slippery surfaces, especially around Marseille, Venice, and Dubrovnik. No marine warnings are in effect but maintain vigilance as winds approach Small Craft Advisory thresholds.
regions.eastern-mediterranean:
Eastern Mediterranean Weather Briefing for March 31, 2026:

Current conditions in Athens/Piraeus show mild temperatures at 59°F with light WSW winds at 2 knots and partly cloudy skies. Visibility is good with a low 3% chance of rain. Expect foggy conditions later today in Athens with light southerly winds increasing to 7 knots; caution advised for small craft navigating nearshore areas due to reduced visibility. By April 1, a shift to northeasterly winds at 11 knots will bring rain showers to Athens and nearby ports including Santorini and Mykonos, with rainfall probability reaching 100 percent; vessel operators should secure loose gear and prepare for wet decks.
